title: festuca halleri subsp. rupicaprina
intro: Festuca halleri subsp. rupicaprina, also known as Balkan fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various health issues. Festuca halleri subsp. rupicaprina has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which are often used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being.
